title: tailtest session
description: Written to .tailtest/session.json at session start. Appended to on each hook fire. Read and cleared by Claude at the start of each user turn.

session_id.description: Unique identifier for this Claude Code session. Format: ISO timestamp + random suffix. Example: 2026-04-13T10-30-00-a1b2c3

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session started. Written once by session_start.py.

project_root.description: Absolute path to the project root as detected by session_start.py.

runners.description: Detected runners per language, keyed by language name. Written once by session_start.py. Used by every subsequent hook fire to select the correct runner without re-scanning.

command.description: The runner executable. Examples: pytest, vitest, phpunit, go, rspec, cargo.

args.description: Default arguments. Examples: ["-q"] for pytest, ["run"] for vitest.

test_location.description: Relative path from project root where tests live. Examples: tests/, __tests__/, spec/

depth.description: Scenario depth. Read from .tailtest/config.json at session start. Defaults to standard if config is absent.

paused.description: When true, the PostToolUse hook exits immediately without queuing files or emitting context. Set by /tailtest off, cleared by /tailtest on. Always false at session start.

report_path.description: Relative path (from project root) where the session report will be written. Set at session start. Format: .tailtest/reports/{session_id}.md

pending_files.description: Files written or edited this turn that have not yet been processed by Claude. Appended to by post_tool_use.py on each hook fire. Read and cleared to [] by Claude at the start of each user turn.

path.description: File path relative to project root.

language.description: Detected language. Examples: python, javascript, typescript, php, go, ruby, rust, java.

status.description: new-file: file was created or first touched in this session -- generate scenarios. legacy-file: file existed before this session -- run existing tests if present, stay silent if not.

touched_files.description: All files touched in this session (relative paths). Used by post_tool_use.py to determine new-file vs legacy-file status on subsequent edits. Also used as the no-git fallback: when .git is absent, any file not in this list on first touch is treated as new-file.

fix_attempts.description: Number of failed fix attempts per file this session. Keyed by relative file path. Incremented by Claude after each failed fix. Reset to 0 when the file passes. Claude stops at 3 and does not attempt a 4th fix.

generated_tests.description: Maps source file path (relative to project_root) to the test file generated for it this session. Written by Claude after each test generation (Step 4). Read by post_tool_use.py to emit 'update existing test' instead of 'write test to' when the same source file is edited again. Reset at session start.

packages.description: Per-package runners for monorepo projects. Keyed by package path relative to project_root (e.g. 'packages/api'). Each value is a runners dict identical in structure to the top-level runners field. Written once by session_start.py when a monorepo is detected. Empty for flat projects.

deferred_failures.description: Failures the user explicitly chose not to fix this session. Not resurfaced unless the relevant file is edited again.

file.description: Relative path to the file with the deferred failure.

description.description: One-line summary of the failure that was deferred.

deferred_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the user chose to defer.
